LAW AND MOTION 302, HEARING ON PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS CONTINUED TO JANUARY 23, 2013 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER VENKANTA PULAKANTI M.D.'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS DENIED. ON THE BASIS OF ITS INDEPENDENT REVIEW OF THE RECORD, THE COURT CONCLUDES THAT RESPONDENT'S FINDINGS ARE SUPPORTED BY THE WEIGHT OF THE EVIDENCE. THE RECORDS OF PATIENT JH ARE ADMISSIBLE AS BUSINESS RECORDS IN LIGHT OF P'S TESTIMONY THAT THEY WERE TRUE AND CORRECT AND HIS MEDICAL ASSISTANT'S TESTIMONY CONCERNING THE OFFICE PRACTICE IN PREPARING THEM. RESPONDENT PROPERLY IMPOSED DISCIPLINE FOR DISHONESTY AND ALTERING MEDICAL RECORDS WITH FRAUDULENT INTENT AS TO PATIENT VP. THE EVIDENCE, INCLUDING PETITIONER'S ADMISSIONS, ESTABLISHES THAT HE KNOWINGLY FALSIFIED THOSE RECORDS IN ORDER TO INFLUENCE THE BOARD AND ITS REVIEW. THE EVIDENCE SUPPORTS THE INFERENCE THAT HE KNEW HIS ENTRIES WERE FALSE, BUT AT MINIMUM, HE ENTERED INFORMATION HE DID NOT KNOW WAS TRUE. RESPONDENT CORRECTLY FOUND THAT TP WAS A PATIENT AT THE TIME OF THE OFFENDING TELEPHONE CALL. THE FIRST PART OF THE CALL IS CLEARLY THAT OF A PATIENT CONSULTING WITH HER PHYSICIAN AND RECEIVING MEDICAL ADVICE. THE PARTIES' STIPULATION DID NOT EXCLUDE TP CONTINUING TO BE A PATIENT AFTER APRIL 4, 2008. GIVEN THE FACTUAL CIRCUMSTANCES, THE PENALTY IMPOSED WAS WITHIN THE BOARD'S DISCRETION. RESPONDENT IS DIRECTED TO PREPARE A STATEMENT OF DECISION CONSISTENT WITH THIS RULING AND RESPONSIVE TO PETITIONER'S ISSUES TO BE ADDRESSED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. STATEMENT OF DECISION SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

calendar.matters:
Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Feb-11-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: CAVAN COX, ESQ., 949.371.5095, FOR PETITIONER VENKANTA PULAKANTI MD.
MATTHEW DAVIS, DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL, 619.645.2093, FOR RESPONDENT THE MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: petitioner venkanta pulakanti m.d.'s petition for Writ Of Mandate is Denied. On the basis of its independent review of the record, the Court concludes that Respondent's findings are supported by the weight of the evidence. The records of Patient JH are admissible as business records in light of P's testimony that they were true and correct and his medical assistant's testimony concerning the office practice in preparing them. Respondent properly imposed discipline for dishonesty and altering medical records with fraudulent intent as to Patient VP. The evidence, including Petitioner's admissions, establishes that he knowingly falsified those records in order to influence the Board and its review. The evidence supports the inference that he knew his entries were false, but at minimum, he entered information he did not know was true. Respondent correctly found that TP was a patient at the time of the offending telephone call. The first part of the call is clearly that of a patient consulting with her physician and receiving medical advice. The parties' stipulation did not exclude TP continuing to be a patient after April 4, 2008. Given the factual circumstances, the penalty imposed was within the Board's discretion. Respondent is directed to prepare a statement of decision consistent with this ruling and responsive to Petitioner's issues to be addressed. Order signed in open Court. statement of decision signed in open Court. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

The opposing party must deliver courtesy copies of its opposition papers to department 302 with a cover letter stating the new hearing date no later than 4pm December 10.
